<input_image_1>
<input_image_2>
A checker game is being played on a grid of 21 squares with 9 green and 11 red checkers. Initially, the checkers are arranged as shown in the starting configuration with the 20 checkers occupying 20 squares and one unoccupied square. Green checkers only move rightward and red checkers only move leftward. Every move is either i) a slide to the adjacent empty square, or ii) a jump over one position to an empty square, provided the checker being jumped over is of a different colour. Each square can accommodate a maximum of one checker at any time. How many moves are required to reach the ending configuration from the starting configuration following the specified rules?

<output_image_1> Step 1: The green checker jumps right from square 6 to square 8. <output_image_2> Step 2: The red checker slides left from square 7 to square 6. <output_image_3> Step 3: The red checker jumps left from square 9 to square 7. <output_image_4> Step 4: The red checker jumps left from square 11 to square 9. Final Answer: 4.

Green checkers only move rightward and red checkers only move leftward. Every move is either i) a slide to the adjacent empty square, or ii) a jump over one position to an empty square, provided the checker being jumped over is of a different colour. Each square can accommodate a maximum of one checker at any time. How many moves are required to reach the ending configuration from the starting configuration following the specified rules?

<output_image_1> Step 1: The red checker jumps left from square 8 to square 6. <output_image_2> Step 2: The red checker jumps left from square 10 to square 8. <output_image_3> Step 3: The red checker slides left from square 11 to square 10. Final Answer: 3.
